name: Koloa Gulch
tagline: A rugged stream-walking adventure to a hidden waterfall deep in the windward mountains.
parking: Limited roadside parking on Kamehameha Highway near the trailhead — be respectful of residential areas
bestTime: Dry conditions are critical — never attempt after heavy rain due to flash flood risk
talkStory: About 5 miles round trip with 800 ft of gradual elevation gain, but the distance is deceptive — you're walking IN the stream for most of it. Expect at least 20 stream crossings, slippery boulders, and overgrown sections. The reward is a beautiful multi-tiered waterfall in a narrow gulch. This trail requires real preparation: water shoes or canyoneering boots, trekking poles, and always check weather before going. Flash floods are a genuine danger here. Verify current trail access before heading out — the trailhead area borders private land.
